text:
In a bustling town far away, there was a peculiar bookstore that nobody seemed to want. It went unnamed; even the locals called it the Bookshop of Shadows. Every evening there was a melancholy owl perched on a statue outside, never calling out but emitting mournful hoots every hour or so.

One day, an old lady went there seeking something to read. She stood at the window looking into the darkness outside, scanning the shelves for anything that would ignite her imagination. That's when she found it—a single volume of lost medieval romantic poetry that seemed to glow with an otherworldly lumosity.

The old lady took it home and fell asleep reading it right away. For several nights in a row—and after midnight she swore she could hear something fluttering outside—the owl's mournful tales filled her dreams.

When dawn came the next morning, she went back. But this time, the owl perched higher than ever.

"Nope," the lady said quietly.

She picked up
